title: Add OpenStack 2026.1 release config and Keystone tests
description:
# GitHub Issue #148: Add OpenStack 2026.1 release configuration and Keystone deployment testing

**Category**: testing | **Scope**: Medium

**Description**: OpenStack 2026.1 has been released. Add a complete release configuration directory `releases/2026.1/` with updated source refs, test refs, upper constraints, extra packages, and test excludes for Keystone. Build and deploy the Keystone 2026.1 image in CI E2E tests to verify that the operator can deploy the new release without code changes to the operator itself. This is a deployment test only — upgrade testing (2025.2 → 2026.1) is out of scope and will be handled separately.

The existing `releases/2025.2/` directory serves as the template. Key changes:
- `source-refs.yaml`: Update `keystone` to the 2026.1 release tag (e.g. `"29.0.0"` — verify against https://releases.openstack.org/2026.1/)
- `test-refs.yaml`: Update `tempest` and `keystone-tempest-plugin` to versions compatible with 2026.1
- `upper-constraints.txt`: Fetch from the OpenStack `requirements` repo for the `stable/2026.1` branch
- `extra-packages.yaml`: Review for any new or removed dependencies in Keystone 2026.1
- `test-excludes/keystone.txt`: Review and update exclusion patterns for tests that are no longer relevant or newly broken

The CI workflows and E2E tests need to support building and testing against 2026.1 in addition to 2025.2. This validates forward compatibility of the operator with the next OpenStack release.

**Motivation**: A key USP of this operator is comprehensive CI testing. Validating that the operator can deploy a new OpenStack release without operator code changes proves release-agnostic design and gives confidence for production upgrades. Early detection of incompatibilities with 2026.1 (new config options, changed defaults, deprecated features, new dependencies) reduces the risk window when customers upgrade.

**Affected Areas**:
- `releases/2026.1/source-refs.yaml` (new)
- `releases/2026.1/test-refs.yaml` (new)
- `releases/2026.1/upper-constraints.txt` (new)
- `releases/2026.1/extra-packages.yaml` (new)
- `releases/2026.1/test-excludes/keystone.txt` (new)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yaml` — E2E and Tempest jobs need a 2026.1 matrix entry or parallel job
- `.github/workflows/build-images.yaml` — image build for 2026.1 tag
- `tests/e2e/keystone/*/00-keystone-cr.yaml` — E2E test CRs referencing image tag
- `hack/run-tempest.sh` — RELEASE default or parameterisation
- `tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh` — validation must cover new release directory

**Acceptance Criteria**:
- [ ] Directory `releases/2026.1/` exists with all 5 configuration files (`source-refs.yaml`, `test-refs.yaml`, `extra-packages.yaml`, `upper-constraints.txt`, `test-excludes/keystone.txt`)
- [ ] `source-refs.yaml` references the correct Keystone git tag for the 2026.1 release
- [ ] `upper-constraints.txt` is sourced from the OpenStack `requirements` repo `stable/2026.1` branch
- [ ] `verify_release_config.sh` passes for the new `releases/2026.1/` directory
- [ ] Keystone 2026.1 container image builds successfully in CI
- [ ] Chainsaw E2E `basic-deployment` test passes with the 2026.1 image (full reconciliation cycle: secrets, database, config, bootstrap, deployment ready)
- [ ] Tempest API tests pass against Keystone deployed from the 2026.1 image
- [ ] Existing 2025.2 CI jobs remain unaffected and continue to pass
- [ ] No operator code changes are required (if code changes are needed, document them as a separate issue)

**Labels:** enhancement

---
Source: https://github.com/C5C3/forge/issues/148

stories.title: CI pipeline automatically builds Keystone 2026.1 container images
stories.want: to automatically discover and build Keystone container images for the 2026.1 release from the releases/2026.1/ configuration
stories.so_that: the operator can be tested against the new OpenStack release without manual build intervention
criteria: releases/2026.1/ directory exists with source-refs.yaml containing keystone: "29.0.0"
build-images.yaml generate-matrix job discovers 2026.1 and includes keystone×2026.1 in the service build matrix
Service image ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2026.1 builds successfully on amd64 and arm64
upper-constraints.txt from stable/2026.1 branch is used during the build

stories.title: CI validates Keystone 2026.1 deployment via Chainsaw E2E test
stories.want: to run the basic-deployment Chainsaw E2E test against a Keystone 2026.1 image in a kind cluster
stories.so_that: forward compatibility of the operator with OpenStack 2026.1 is verified without operator code changes
criteria: tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/ directory exists with 00-keystone-cr.yaml using tag: "2026.1"
ci.yaml e2e-operator job builds and loads keystone:2026.1 into kind alongside keystone:2025.2
Chainsaw basic-deployment-2026-1 test passes: all 5 sub-conditions True, Ready=True, Deployment available, Service on port 5000, API accessible at /v3
Existing 2025.2 E2E tests continue to pass in the same job run

stories.title: CI validates Keystone 2026.1 API compatibility via Tempest
stories.want: to run Tempest API integration tests against Keystone deployed from the 2026.1 image
stories.so_that: API compatibility between the operator's configuration and Keystone 2026.1 is confirmed
criteria: tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1/ directory exists with 00-keystone-cr.yaml, tempest.conf, include-tests.txt, and exclude-tests.txt
ci.yaml tempest job runs as a release matrix with entries for 2025.2 and 2026.1
Tempest API tests (tempest.api.identity, keystone_tempest_plugin.tests) pass against 2026.1 Keystone
Tempest image is built with 2026.1-compatible tempest and keystone-tempest-plugin versions from test-refs.yaml

stories.title: Release validation script covers all release directories
stories.want: verify_release_config.sh to dynamically validate all releases/*/ directories instead of only 2025.2
stories.so_that: configuration errors in any release directory are caught early and automatically
criteria: Tests 1, 2, and 4 in verify_release_config.sh iterate over all releases/*/ directories
verify_release_config.sh passes for both releases/2025.2/ and releases/2026.1/
Adding a new release directory automatically includes it in validation without script changes
Existing test assertions (YAML validity, semver check, structure check, Dockerfile wiring) are preserved

stories.title: Existing 2025.2 CI jobs remain unaffected
stories.want: all existing 2025.2 CI jobs (E2E, Tempest, image builds, verification) to continue passing without modifications
stories.so_that: stability of the existing release is maintained while adding 2026.1 support
criteria: All 15 existing Keystone E2E test scenarios pass with 2025.2 images
Tempest API tests pass against 2025.2 Keystone deployment
build-images.yaml continues to build keystone:2025.2 service images
verify-container-images.yaml verification tests pass for 2025.2 config

stories.title: Build pipeline produces per-release Tempest images
stories.want: to build Tempest container images per OpenStack release with release-specific test tool versions
stories.so_that: each release is tested with compatible Tempest and plugin versions rather than a single hardcoded version
criteria: build-images.yaml Tempest build jobs use dynamic release discovery instead of hardcoded 2025.2
Tempest image for 2025.2 uses tempest 45.0.0 and keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0
Tempest image for 2026.1 uses versions from releases/2026.1/test-refs.yaml
Per-release Tempest images receive the same supply chain security treatment (SBOM, signing, attestation)

requirements.description: The releases/2026.1/ directory SHALL contain all 5 required configuration files: source-refs.yaml, test-refs.yaml, extra-packages.yaml, upper-constraints.txt, and test-excludes/keystone.txt
requirements.rationale: Complete release configuration is required for the build pipeline to discover and build 2026.1 images, and for verification scripts to validate the configuration

scenarios.name: All 5 files present
scenarios.when: the releases/2026.1/ directory is inspected
scenarios.then: all 5 files exist: source-refs.yaml, test-refs.yaml, extra-packages.yaml, upper-constraints.txt, test-excludes/keystone.txt
and_then: each file has an SPDX Apache-2.0 license header
each file is valid YAML or valid text format

scenarios.name: Missing file detected
scenarios.when: any of the 5 required files is absent from releases/2026.1/
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh reports a FAIL for the missing file
and_then: the verification script exits with non-zero status

scenarios.name: Build pipeline discovers 2026.1
scenarios.when: build-images.yaml generate-matrix job runs
scenarios.then: the releases/2026.1/source-refs.yaml is parsed and keystone×2026.1 is included in the build matrix
and_then: the matrix output contains both 2025.2 and 2026.1 entries

requirements.description: source-refs.yaml SHALL reference the correct Keystone git tag for the OpenStack 2026.1 release (expected: 29.0.0)
requirements.rationale: The source ref determines which upstream Keystone code is cloned and built into the container image; an incorrect tag would build the wrong version

scenarios.name: Valid semver tag
scenarios.when: source-refs.yaml is parsed by verify_release_config.sh
scenarios.then: the keystone value matches the semver pattern ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$
and_then: the version is reported as PASS in the verification output

scenarios.name: Tag exists in upstream repo
scenarios.when: hack/ci-build-service-image.sh clones openstack/keystone at the specified tag
scenarios.then: the git clone succeeds with depth 1
and_then: the cloned code corresponds to the 2026.1 release

scenarios.name: Missing or null keystone key
scenarios.when: source-refs.yaml lacks a keystone key or its value is null
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh reports FAIL: keystone key is missing
and_then: the build script exits with an error before attempting to clone

requirements.description: upper-constraints.txt SHALL be sourced from the OpenStack requirements repository stable/2026.1 branch
requirements.rationale: Upper constraints pin all transitive Python dependency versions for reproducible builds; using the wrong branch would cause version mismatches or build failures

scenarios.name: File contains valid constraints
scenarios.when: upper-constraints.txt is inspected
scenarios.then: the file is non-empty, contains lines matching the pattern package===version, and has 400+ constraint lines
and_then: key packages like pbr, oslo.config, keystonemiddleware are present

scenarios.name: Constraints are used during image build
scenarios.when: the service image build runs with upper-constraints=releases/2026.1/
scenarios.then: pip installs dependencies respecting the constraint pins
and_then: the build completes without version conflict errors

scenarios.name: Constraint overrides applied if present
scenarios.when: overrides/2026.1/constraints.txt exists
scenarios.then: scripts/apply-constraint-overrides.sh merges overrides into upper-constraints.txt before the build
and_then: overridden packages use the specified version

requirements.description: verify_release_config.sh SHALL validate all release directories under releases/*/ dynamically, not just a hardcoded release
requirements.rationale: The current script hardcodes releases/2025.2/ in tests 1, 2, and 4, meaning new releases are not validated; dynamic iteration ensures all releases are covered

scenarios.name: Both releases validated
scenarios.when: verify_release_config.sh runs with both releases/2025.2/ and releases/2026.1/ present
scenarios.then: both directories are validated with all applicable tests
and_then: the output shows PASS/FAIL for each release directory separately

scenarios.name: New release auto-discovered
scenarios.when: a releases/2027.1/ directory is added in the future
scenarios.then: verify_release_config.sh includes it in validation without script changes
and_then: no hardcoded release version strings remain in the script

scenarios.name: Single release still works
scenarios.when: only releases/2025.2/ exists (2026.1 not yet added)
scenarios.then: the script validates 2025.2 and exits 0
and_then: no errors about missing releases

requirements.description: The Keystone 2026.1 container image SHALL build successfully in the CI e2e-operator and build-images pipelines
requirements.rationale: A successful image build is a prerequisite for all deployment and API testing; build failures must be detected and reported clearly

scenarios.name: CI e2e-operator image build succeeds
scenarios.when: ci.yaml e2e-operator job runs hack/ci-build-service-image.sh with RELEASE=2026.1
scenarios.then: the image ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2026.1 is built and tagged
and_then: the image is loaded into the kind cluster successfully

scenarios.name: Build pipeline image build succeeds
scenarios.when: build-images.yaml build-service-images job runs for keystone×2026.1
scenarios.then: the multi-platform image is built for amd64 and arm64
and_then: the image is pushed by digest to GHCR

scenarios.name: Build failure produces clear error
scenarios.when: the 2026.1 image build fails due to missing dependency or constraint conflict
scenarios.then: the CI job fails with a descriptive error message identifying the failure point
and_then: the 2025.2 image build is not affected by the failure

requirements.description: The Chainsaw basic-deployment E2E test SHALL pass with the Keystone 2026.1 image, verifying the full reconciliation cycle
requirements.rationale: The basic-deployment test validates that the operator can deploy a Keystone instance from a new release image without operator code changes, proving release-agnostic design

scenarios.name: Full reconciliation succeeds
scenarios.when: the basic-deployment-2026-1 Chainsaw test applies a Keystone CR with tag: "2026.1"
scenarios.then: all 5 sub-conditions (SecretsReady, FernetKeysReady, DatabaseReady, DeploymentReady, BootstrapReady) reach True
and_then: the aggregate Ready condition is True with reason AllReady

scenarios.name: API is accessible
scenarios.when: the Keystone 2026.1 deployment reaches Ready state
scenarios.then: a curl test pod can successfully reach http://keystone-2026-1-api.openstack.svc:5000/v3
and_then: the response contains valid JSON with the Keystone v3 API version info

scenarios.name: Owned resources are created
scenarios.when: the Keystone 2026.1 CR is reconciled
scenarios.then: Deployment, Service, CronJob, Secret, RBAC, PushSecret, ConfigMap resources exist
and_then: the Deployment has available replicas > 0

requirements.description: Tempest API integration tests SHALL pass against Keystone deployed from the 2026.1 image
requirements.rationale: Tempest tests validate API compatibility at the protocol level, catching regressions in config defaults, authentication flows, and endpoint behavior that E2E tests may miss

scenarios.name: Identity API tests pass
scenarios.when: Tempest runs against a Keystone 2026.1 deployment with the keystone-2026-1 config
scenarios.then: tempest.api.identity tests pass (excluding patterns in exclude-tests.txt)
and_then: JUnit XML results are uploaded as CI artifacts

scenarios.name: Plugin tests pass
scenarios.when: Tempest runs with keystone-tempest-plugin against Keystone 2026.1
scenarios.then: keystone_tempest_plugin.tests pass (excluding LDAP, federation, OAuth2 patterns)
and_then: no unexpected test failures outside the exclude list

scenarios.name: Tempest image uses 2026.1 versions
scenarios.when: hack/ci-build-tempest-image.sh runs with RELEASE=2026.1
scenarios.then: the Tempest image installs tempest and keystone-tempest-plugin versions from releases/2026.1/test-refs.yaml
and_then: the versions differ from 2025.2 if upstream published new releases

requirements.description: Existing 2025.2 CI jobs SHALL remain unaffected and continue to pass after all changes
requirements.rationale: Stability of the existing release pipeline is critical; 2026.1 support must be additive without regressing 2025.2

scenarios.name: 2025.2 E2E tests pass
scenarios.when: ci.yaml e2e-operator job runs all Chainsaw tests in tests/e2e/keystone/
scenarios.then: all 15 existing test scenarios (basic-deployment, autoscaling, scale, etc.) pass with 2025.2 images
and_then: no existing test is broken by the addition of 2026.1 resources

scenarios.name: 2025.2 Tempest tests pass
scenarios.when: ci.yaml tempest job runs with release=2025.2 matrix entry
scenarios.then: Tempest API tests pass against 2025.2 Keystone with unchanged configuration
and_then: 2025.2 Tempest results are uploaded with a release-specific artifact name

scenarios.name: 2025.2 image builds unaffected
scenarios.when: build-images.yaml runs after adding releases/2026.1/
scenarios.then: keystone:2025.2 service image continues to build successfully
and_then: the 2025.2 build uses releases/2025.2/ configuration exclusively

requirements.description: The build-images.yaml Tempest pipeline SHOULD support per-release Tempest image builds instead of hardcoding releases/2025.2/
requirements.rationale: Different OpenStack releases may require different Tempest and plugin versions; hardcoding a single release prevents testing newer releases with compatible tooling in the production build pipeline

scenarios.name: Tempest builds per release
scenarios.when: build-images.yaml runs with releases/2025.2/ and releases/2026.1/ present
scenarios.then: separate Tempest images are built for each release using the respective test-refs.yaml versions
and_then: each image has release-specific version tags

scenarios.name: Dynamic release discovery for Tempest
scenarios.when: the generate-matrix job runs
scenarios.then: a tempest-matrix output is generated with release×platform combinations for all releases
and_then: build-tempest and merge-tempest-image consume this matrix

scenarios.name: Backward compatible with single release
scenarios.when: only releases/2025.2/ exists
scenarios.then: the Tempest pipeline builds exactly one image matching current behavior
and_then: no regression from the parameterization change

requirements.description: releases/2026.1/test-excludes/keystone.txt SHALL have valid stestr exclude-list format with documented exclusion patterns
requirements.rationale: Invalid exclude patterns would cause stestr to fail silently or exclude wrong tests; documentation ensures exclusions are intentional and reviewable

scenarios.name: Valid format validated
scenarios.when: verify_release_config.sh runs test 5 against releases/2026.1/test-excludes/keystone.txt
scenarios.then: the file passes: non-empty, no whitespace-only lines, contains comment lines
and_then: every non-blank line is either a comment or a valid regex pattern

scenarios.name: Filename matches service key
scenarios.when: verify_release_config.sh runs test 7 against releases/2026.1/
scenarios.then: keystone.txt matches the keystone key in source-refs.yaml
and_then: no orphaned exclude files exist for services not in source-refs.yaml

scenarios.name: Exclusions are documented
scenarios.when: test-excludes/keystone.txt is reviewed
scenarios.then: each exclusion pattern has a preceding comment explaining why it is excluded
and_then: the comment references the specific test category or CI limitation

tasks.title: Create releases/2026.1/source-refs.yaml and test-refs.yaml with correct 2026.1 version pins (REQ-001, REQ-002)
tasks.description: Create releases/2026.1/ directory. Add source-refs.yaml with keystone: "29.0.0" (verify tag against https://releases.openstack.org/2026.1/). Add test-refs.yaml with compatible tempest and keystone-tempest-plugin versions for 2026.1 (check PyPI for latest compatible releases). Both files must have SPDX Apache-2.0 headers matching the 2025.2 format. Follow exact YAML structure from releases/2025.2/source-refs.yaml and releases/2025.2/test-refs.yaml.

tasks.title: Fetch releases/2026.1/upper-constraints.txt from OpenStack requirements stable/2026.1 branch (REQ-001, REQ-003)
tasks.description: Download upper-constraints.txt from https://opendev.org/openstack/requirements/raw/branch/stable/2026.1/upper-constraints.txt and save as releases/2026.1/upper-constraints.txt. Verify the file is non-empty and contains 400+ constraint lines. Check that key packages (pbr, oslo.config, keystonemiddleware, python-keystoneclient) are present. If the stable/2026.1 branch does not exist yet, use the master branch constraints and document this in a comment.

tasks.title: Create releases/2026.1/extra-packages.yaml and test-excludes/keystone.txt (REQ-001, REQ-010)
tasks.description: Copy releases/2025.2/extra-packages.yaml to releases/2026.1/extra-packages.yaml. Review Keystone 2026.1 release notes for new or removed dependencies (pip_extras, pip_packages, apt_packages). Create releases/2026.1/test-excludes/keystone.txt by copying from 2025.2. Review exclusion patterns: LDAP, federation, and fernet timing exclusions likely still apply. Add comment header referencing CC-0051 and 2026.1. Both files must have SPDX Apache-2.0 headers.

tasks.title: Refactor verify_release_config.sh tests 1, 2, and 4 to iterate over all releases/*/ directories (REQ-004)
tasks.description: In tests/container-images/verify_release_config.sh, refactor test_source_refs_valid_yaml_with_keystone (test 1, line 22), test_extra_packages_valid_yaml_structure (test 2, line 59), and test_no_hardcoded_apt_packages (test 4, line 199) to iterate over all releases/*/ directories using the same glob pattern as tests 5-7. Replace hardcoded 'releases/2025.2/' paths with a for loop: for release_dir in "$PROJECT_ROOT"/releases/*/; do ... done. Each iteration should report the release name in output. Preserve all existing assertions. Verify the script passes for both 2025.2 and 2026.1.

tasks.title: Create tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/ with Keystone CR and chainsaw-test.yaml (REQ-006)
tasks.description: Create tests/e2e/keystone/basic-deployment-2026-1/00-keystone-cr.yaml by copying from basic-deployment/00-keystone-cr.yaml. Change image tag from "2025.2" to "2026.1". Change metadata.name from keystone-basic to keystone-2026-1 (unique within the openstack namespace). Create chainsaw-test.yaml modeled on basic-deployment/chainsaw-test.yaml: same 6-step structure (apply CR, assert conditions, assert Deployment/Service/PDB, assert Fernet resources, assert ConfigMap, assert API accessibility). Update all resource name references from keystone-basic to keystone-2026-1. Update Step 6 curl test image to ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2026.1. Add SPDX headers and CC-0051 feature reference.

tasks.title: Create tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1/ with Keystone CR, tempest.conf, and include/exclude lists (REQ-007)
tasks.description: Create tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1/ directory. Copy tests/tempest/keystone/00-keystone-cr.yaml, changing image tag to "2026.1" and keeping name as keystone-tempest (safe because Tempest matrix entries run in separate kind clusters). Copy tempest.conf, include-tests.txt, and exclude-tests.txt from tests/tempest/keystone/. Review exclude-tests.txt for 2026.1 compatibility: the RBAC exclusions for keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0 may need adjustment if using a newer plugin version. Add SPDX headers and CC-0051 references. The tempest.conf service URL (keystone-tempest-api.openstack.svc:5000) remains the same.

tasks.title: Update ci.yaml e2e-operator job to build and load Keystone 2026.1 image alongside 2025.2 (REQ-005, REQ-006, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In .github/workflows/ci.yaml e2e-operator job (line 316): (1) After the existing 'Build service image' step (line 340), add a new step 'Build service image (2026.1)' that calls hack/ci-build-service-image.sh with RELEASE=2026.1 env var. (2) In the 'Load images into kind' step (line 346), add: kind load docker-image ${{ env.IMAGE_PREFIX }}/${{ matrix.operator }}:2026.1 --name forge-e2e. Keep existing 2025.2 and 2025.2-upgraded image loading unchanged. The new basic-deployment-2026-1/ test directory will be auto-discovered by chainsaw test tests/e2e/${{ matrix.operator }}/. Increase timeout-minutes from 45 to 55 to account for additional image build time.

tasks.title: Restructure ci.yaml tempest job with release matrix for 2025.2 and 2026.1 (REQ-005, REQ-007, REQ-008)
tasks.description: In .github/workflows/ci.yaml tempest job (line 384): (1) Add strategy.matrix with include entries for release 2025.2 (keystone_cr: tests/tempest/keystone/00-keystone-cr.yaml, config_dir: tests/tempest/keystone) and 2026.1 (keystone_cr: tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1/00-keystone-cr.yaml, config_dir: tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1). (2) Parameterize 'Build service image' step with RELEASE=${{ matrix.release }}. (3) Parameterize 'Build Tempest image' step with RELEASE=${{ matrix.release }}. (4) Update 'Load images into kind' to use ${{ matrix.release }} tag. (5) Update 'Deploy Keystone CR' to use ${{ matrix.keystone_cr }}. (6) Add CONFIG_DIR=${{ matrix.config_dir }} env to 'Run Tempest API tests'. (7) Change artifact names to include release: tempest-results-${{ matrix.release }}. Each matrix entry runs independently with its own kind cluster.

tasks.title: Parameterize build-images.yaml Tempest pipeline for multi-release support (REQ-009)
tasks.description: In .github/workflows/build-images.yaml: (1) Extend generate-matrix job (line 697) to output a tempest-matrix: iterate releases/*/ and generate release×platform pairs (same pattern as build-matrix but without service dimension). Add releases output as a JSON array of release names. (2) Update build-tempest job (line 457): change strategy.matrix to use fromJson(needs.generate-matrix.outputs.tempest-matrix). Replace hardcoded releases/2025.2/ with releases/${{ matrix.release }}/ in version resolution (lines 485-486) and upper-constraints build context (line 532). Add matrix.release to cache scope and artifact names. (3) Update merge-tempest-image job (line 583): add strategy.matrix from generate-matrix releases. Replace hardcoded releases/2025.2/ in version resolution (line 606). Update artifact download pattern and manifest tags to include release. Ensure version tags remain main-branch-only gated.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md 'Adding a New Release' section (REQ-001)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md section 'Adding a New Release' (line 1093): (1) Add test-refs.yaml to the required files list in the directory tree (currently missing). (2) Correct step 2 'Extend the matrix': the generate-matrix job now dynamically discovers releases from releases/*/ — remove the manual matrix edit instruction and explain that adding the release directory is sufficient. (3) Add a note about test-refs.yaml being required for Tempest image builds. (4) Update the file tree example to show all 5 files including test-refs.yaml and test-excludes/. Reference CC-0051.

tasks.title: Update docs/reference/ci-workflow.md and tempest-test-infrastructure.md for multi-release testing (REQ-006, REQ-007)
tasks.description: In docs/reference/ci-workflow.md: document the multi-release E2E testing approach (e2e-operator builds both 2025.2 and 2026.1 images, basic-deployment-2026-1 test added) and the Tempest release matrix (2025.2 and 2026.1 matrix entries). In docs/reference/tempest-test-infrastructure.md: update the File Locations table to include tests/tempest/keystone-2026-1/ and releases/2026.1/test-refs.yaml. Update the Architecture diagram to show per-release Tempest image builds. Add a 'Multi-Release Testing' section explaining the release matrix approach. Reference CC-0051.

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline automatically builds Keystone 2026.1 container images
test_specifications.expected: [validation] source-refs.yaml in releases/2026.1/ is valid YAML with keystone key containing a valid semver version (29.0.0)

test_specifications.story: Existing 2025.2 CI jobs remain unaffected
test_specifications.expected: [validation] source-refs.yaml in releases/2025.2/ continues to pass validation after script refactoring

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline automatically builds Keystone 2026.1 container images
test_specifications.expected: [validation] extra-packages.yaml in releases/2026.1/ has valid structure: pip_extras >= 1, apt_packages >= 1, naming patterns valid

test_specifications.story: Existing 2025.2 CI jobs remain unaffected
test_specifications.expected: [validation] extra-packages.yaml in releases/2025.2/ continues to pass validation after script refactoring

test_specifications.story: Release validation script covers all release directories
test_specifications.expected: [validation] Dockerfile uses ${EXTRA_APT_PACKAGES} build arg (checked once per release directory)

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline automatically builds Keystone 2026.1 container images
test_specifications.expected: [validation] test-excludes/keystone.txt in releases/2026.1/ has valid stestr format: non-empty, no whitespace-only lines, contains comments

test_specifications.story: CI pipeline automatically builds Keystone 2026.1 container images

review_criteria: All 5 files in releases/2026.1/ exist with correct SPDX headers, valid YAML/text format, and content matching the 2025.2 template structure
verify_release_config.sh passes for both releases/2025.2/ and releases/2026.1/ with zero FAIL results; no hardcoded release paths remain in tests 1, 2, or 4
ci.yaml e2e-operator job successfully builds and loads both keystone:2025.2 and keystone:2026.1 images; all existing 2025.2 E2E tests pass alongside the new basic-deployment-2026-1 test
ci.yaml tempest job release matrix produces independent runs for 2025.2 and 2026.1 with unique artifact names; the 2025.2 entry behaves identically to the pre-change single-release job
build-images.yaml Tempest pipeline is parameterized: no hardcoded releases/2025.2/ references remain in build-tempest or merge-tempest-image jobs; version resolution uses matrix.release
No operator Go code changes are required — all changes are limited to releases/, tests/, hack/, .github/, and docs/ directories
shellcheck passes for verify_release_config.sh and all hack/*.sh scripts without warnings
Documentation in docs/reference/ accurately reflects the multi-release architecture: build-images-workflow.md 'Adding a New Release' includes test-refs.yaml and notes dynamic matrix discovery
implementation_notes:
## Architecture Decision: Additive Multi-Release Support

All changes are additive — no existing file contents are modified except to replace hardcoded release references with dynamic iteration. The operator Go code is explicitly unchanged (acceptance criteria).

## Data Flow: 2026.1 Image Build and Test

```
releases/2026.1/source-refs.yaml (keystone: "29.0.0")
  → hack/ci-build-service-image.sh (RELEASE=2026.1)
  → Docker build: ghcr.io/c5c3/keystone:2026.1
  → kind load docker-image
  ├→ E2E: basic-deployment-2026-1/chainsaw-test.yaml
  └→ Tempest: keystone-2026-1/tempest.conf → Tempest API tests

releases/2026.1/test-refs.yaml
  → hack/ci-build-tempest-image.sh (RELEASE=2026.1)
  → Docker build: c5c3/tempest:local
  → Tempest API tests against keystone:2026.1

releases/2026.1/upper-constraints.txt
  → Docker build context (upper-constraints=releases/2026.1/)
  → Pins all Python dependency versions during pip install
```

## Key Patterns Followed

1. **Dynamic matrix generation** (build-images.yaml:711-750): generate-matrix iterates releases/*/ to auto-discover services. Adding releases/2026.1/ is sufficient for service image builds — no manual matrix edit needed.

2. **Release-parameterized scripts** (hack/ci-build-service-image.sh, hack/ci-build-tempest-image.sh): Scripts accept RELEASE env var with default 2025.2. CI passes explicit RELEASE values.

3. **Glob-based validation** (verify_release_config.sh tests 5-7): Tests 5, 6, 7 already iterate releases/*/test-excludes/. Tests 1, 2, 4 need the same treatment.

4. **E2E test isolation**: Each Chainsaw test uses a unique CR name in the openstack namespace. The 2026.1 basic-deployment uses name keystone-2026-1 to avoid conflict with keystone-basic.

5. **Tempest matrix isolation**: Each tempest matrix entry gets its own kind cluster, so CR names can be reused (keystone-tempest) across releases.

## Potential Pitfalls

1. **Keystone 29.0.0 tag may not exist**: Verify against https://releases.openstack.org/2026.1/ before implementing. Fall back to the stable/2026.1 branch if the tag is not yet published.

2. **stable/2026.1 branch availability**: The upper-constraints.txt depends on the openstack/requirements stable/2026.1 branch existing. If unavailable, use the master branch constraints temporarily.

3. **Tempest/plugin version compatibility**: The 2026.1 test-refs.yaml versions need to be verified against PyPI. Newer keystone-tempest-plugin versions may fix RBAC test exclusions or introduce new failures.

4. **CI timeout increase**: Building two service images in e2e-operator adds ~5-10 minutes. The timeout should be increased from 45 to 55 minutes.

5. **Tempest RBAC exclusions**: The exclude-tests.txt patterns for RBAC tests (keystone-tempest-plugin 0.19.0 vs Keystone 2025.2 policy mismatches) may need adjustment for 2026.1 if using a newer plugin version.

6. **Documentation discrepancy**: docs/reference/build-images-workflow.md 'Adding a New Release' section incorrectly says to manually extend the build matrix. The generate-matrix job now handles this dynamically. Must be corrected.
